### Primary and secondary education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Primary and secondary education")]

Schools in Thiruvananthapuram are classified as aided, unaided and Government schools.[[240]](#cite_note-240)[[241]](#cite_note-scllist-241) The government schools are run directly by the [Kerala State Education Board](/wiki/Kerala_State_Education_Board "Kerala State Education Board") and follow the syllabus prescribed by the state government.[[242]](#cite_note-syllabus-242) The aided schools also follow the [state syllabus](/wiki/Kerala_State_Education_Board "Kerala State Education Board"). [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") and English are the primary languages of instruction; [Tamil](/wiki/Tamil_language "Tamil language") and [Hindi](/wiki/Hindi "Hindi") are also used.[[242]](#cite_note-syllabus-242) The schools are affiliated with [The State Council of Educational Research and Training](/wiki/Kerala_State_Education_Board "Kerala State Education Board") (SCERT), [Central Board of Secondary Education](/wiki/Central_Board_of_Secondary_Education "Central Board of Secondary Education") (CBSE), [Indian Certificate of Secondary Education](/wiki/Indian_Certificate_of_Secondary_Education "Indian Certificate of Secondary Education") (ICSE), [International General Certificate of Secondary Education](/wiki/International_General_Certificate_of_Secondary_Education "International General Certificate of Secondary Education") (IGCSE) and [National Institute of Open Schooling](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Open_Schooling "National Institute of Open Schooling") (NIOS). In the National Achievement Survey conducted by the [National Council of Educational Research and Training](/wiki/National_Council_of_Educational_Research_and_Training "National Council of Educational Research and Training") (NCERT), Thiruvananthapuram is ranked as the best city in [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala").[[243]](#cite_note-243)[[244]](#cite_note-ncertsurv-244)

The notable schools in the city include [St. Mary's Higher Secondary School](/wiki/St._Mary%27s_Higher_Secondary_School_(Thiruvananthapuram) "St. Mary's Higher Secondary School (Thiruvananthapuram)"), which is considered one of the largest schools in Asia, with the total number of students exceeding 12,000,[[245]](#cite_note-245)[[246]](#cite_note-246) [Government Model Boys Higher Secondary School](/wiki/Government_Model_Boys_Higher_Secondary_School "Government Model Boys Higher Secondary School"), [Government Higher Secondary School for Girls](/wiki/Government_Higher_Secondary_School_for_Girls "Government Higher Secondary School for Girls"), [Holy Angel's Convent Trivandrum](/wiki/Holy_Angel%27s_Convent_Trivandrum "Holy Angel's International School"), [Chinmaya Vidyalayas](/wiki/Chinmaya_Vidyalayas "Chinmaya Vidyalayas"), [Kendriya Vidyalaya](/wiki/Kendriya_Vidyalaya "Kendriya Vidyalaya"), [Loyola School](/wiki/Loyola_School,_Thiruvananthapuram "Loyola School, Thiruvananthapuram"), [Christ Nagar School, Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Christ_Nagar_School,_Thiruvananthapuram "Christ Nagar School, ### Higher education and research

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Higher education and research")]

Thiruvananthapuram is a major educational and research hub with various institutions in the fields of [space science](/wiki/Space_science "Space science"), [information technology](/wiki/Information_technology "Information technology"), [physical science](/wiki/Outline_of_physical_science "Outline of physical science"), [biotechnology](/wiki/Bio-technology "Bio-technology"), [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering") and [medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine"). There are three universities in Thiruvananthapuram: two [state universities](/wiki/State_university_(India) "State university (India)") and one [deemed university](/wiki/Deemed_university "Deemed university"). The state universities are the [University of Kerala](/wiki/University_of_Kerala "University of Kerala") and [APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University](/wiki/APJ_Abdul_Kalam_Technological_University "APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University"). [Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Space_Science_and_Technology "Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology") (IIST), is a government-aided institute and deemed university.[[248]](#cite_note-248) IIST is the first of its kind in the country, to offer [graduate courses](/wiki/Academic_degree "Academic degree") and [research](/wiki/Research "Research") in space sciences, space technology and space applications.[[249]](#cite_note-iist-249) The city also houses two [Institutes of National Importance](/wiki/List_of_Institutes_of_National_Importance "List of Institutes of National Importance"); [Sree Chitra Tirunal Institute for Medical Sciences and Technology](/wiki/Sree_Chitra_Tirunal_Institute_for_Medical_Sciences_and_Technology "Sree Chitra Tirunal Institute for Medical Sciences and Technology") (SCTIMST) and [Indian Institute of Science Education and Research](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Science_Education_and_Research,_Thiruvananthapuram "Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Thiruvananthapuram") (IISER).[[250]](#cite_note-inilist-250) Thiruvananthapuram is one of the regional headquarters of [Indira Gandhi National Open University](/wiki/Indira_Gandhi_National_Open_University "Indira Gandhi National Open University") (IGNOU).[[251]](#cite_note-251)

The [Government Medical College, Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Government_Medical_College,_Thiruvananthapuram "Government Medical College, Thiruvananthapuram") is the first and a premier [medical school](/wiki/Medical_school "Medical school") in Kerala, founded in 1951.[[253]](#cite_note-253) Other notable medical schools apart from [SCTIMST](/wiki/Sree_Chitra_Tirunal_Institute_for_Medical_Sciences_and_Technology "Sree Chitra Tirunal Institute for Medical Sciences and Technology") (which provides super-specialty courses in cardiac and neuroscience) and [Regional Cancer Centre, Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Regional_Cancer_Centre,_Thiruvananthapuram "Regional Cancer Centre, Thiruvananthapuram") (which provides PG courses in radiotherapy and pathology, and super-specialty courses) includes [SUT Academy of Medical Sciences](/wiki/SUT_Academy_of_Medical_Sciences "SUT Academy of Medical Sciences"), Sree Gokulam Medical College and Government Ayurveda College.[[254]](#cite_note-procollist-254)

Other prominent [undergraduate](/wiki/Undergraduate "Undergraduate") and [postgraduate](/wiki/Postgraduate "Postgraduate") colleges include the [Government Arts College](/wiki/Government_Arts_College,_Thiruvananthapuram "Government Arts College, Thiruvananthapuram"), [Mahatma Gandhi College](/wiki/Mahatma_Gandhi_College "Mahatma Gandhi College"), [Mar Ivanios College](/wiki/Mar_Ivanios_College "Mar Ivanios College"), [Government Sanskrit College](/wiki/Government_Sanskrit_College,_Thiruvananthapuram "Government Sanskrit College, Thiruvananthapuram"), [Loyola College of Social Sciences](/wiki/Loyola_College_of_Social_Sciences "Loyola College of Social Sciences"), [St. Xavier's College](/wiki/St._Xavier%27s_College,_Thumba "St. Xavier's College, Thumba") and [All Saints College](/wiki/All_Saints_College,_Thiruvananthapuram "All Saints College, Thiruvananthapuram").[[270]](#cite_note-270) Major [fine arts](/wiki/Fine_art "Fine art") colleges are [Swathi Thirunal College of Music](/wiki/Swathi_Thirunal_College_of_Music "Swathi Thirunal College of Music"), which is the first music academy in Kerala[[271]](#cite_note-271) and [College of Fine Arts Trivandrum](/wiki/College_of_Fine_Arts_Trivandrum "College of Fine Arts Trivandrum").[[272]](#cite_note-272) The [Lakshmibai National College of Physical Education](/wiki/Lakshmibai_National_College_of_Physical_Education "Lakshmibai National College of Physical Education") is one of the two [physical education](/wiki/Physical_education "Physical education") academic institutes of the [Sports Authority of India](/wiki/Sports_Authority_of_India "Sports Authority of India") (SAI).[[273]](#cite_note-273)

The premier research institutes in Thiruvananthapuram include: [Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management, Kerala](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Information_Technology_and_Management,_Kerala "Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management, Kerala") (IIITMK),[[274]](#cite_note-274) [National Institute of Speech and Hearing](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Speech_and_Hearing "National Institute of Speech and Hearing") (NISH),[[275]](#cite_note-275) [Rajiv Gandhi Centre for Biotechnology](/wiki/Rajiv_Gandhi_Centre_for_Biotechnology "Rajiv Gandhi Centre for Biotechnology"),[[276]](#cite_note-276) [Centre for Development of Imaging Technology](/wiki/Centre_for_Development_of_Imaging_Technology "Centre for Development of Imaging Technology") (C-Dit),[[277]](#cite_note-277) [Centre for Development Studies](/wiki/Centre_for_Development_Studies "Centre for Development Studies") (CDS),[[278]](#cite_note-278) [Jawaharlal Nehru Tropical Botanic Garden and Research Institute](/wiki/Jawaharlal_Nehru_Tropical_Botanic_Garden_and_Research_Institute "Jawaharlal Nehru Tropical Botanic Garden and Research Institute"),[[279]](#cite_note-279) [National Centre for Earth Science Studies](/wiki/National_Centre_for_Earth_Science_Studies "National Centre for Earth Science Studies") (NCESS),[[280]](#cite_note-280) [Centre for Development of Advanced Computing](/wiki/C-DAC_Thiruvananthapuram "C-DAC Thiruvananthapuram") (C-DAC)[[281]](#cite_note-281) and [Oriental Research Institute & Manuscripts Library](/wiki/Oriental_Research_Institute_%26_Manuscripts_Library "Oriental Research Institute & Manuscripts Library").[[282]](#cite_note-282)

[Kerala University](/wiki/Kerala_University "Kerala University") is ranked as the best university in Kerala according to the [MHRD](/wiki/Ministry_of_Human_Resource_Development "Ministry of Human Resource Development")'s [National Institutional Ranking Framework](/wiki/National_Institutional_Ranking_Framework "National Institutional Ranking Framework") (NIRF).[[283]](#cite_note-283) Kerala University also ranked top in overall institution rankings in Kerala.[[284]](#cite_note-284) In engineering, [Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Space_Science_and_Technology "Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology") (IIST) is ranked as the best in Kerala and [College of Engineering, Trivandrum](/wiki/College_of_Engineering,_Trivandrum "College of Engineering, Trivandrum") (CET) is ranked third in Kerala.[[285]](#cite_note-285) [College of Engineering, Trivandrum](/wiki/College_of_Engineering,_Trivandrum "College of Engineering, Trivandrum") is also ranked fourth in India and first in Kerala in architecture institution rankings.[[286]](#cite_note-286) The [University College](/wiki/University_College_Thiruvananthapuram "University College Thiruvananthapuram") is listed as the best college in Kerala.[[287]](#cite_note-287)[[288]](#cite_note-288)

## Space and aerospace

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Space and aerospace")]

Thiruvananthapuram is the birthplace of India's space operations.[[289]](#cite_note-auto-289) The first rocket launch in India occurred in Thiruvananthapuram in 1963 at the [Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station](/wiki/Thumba_Equatorial_Rocket_Launching_Station "Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station") (TERLS). Since then, the city has emerged as a major hub for space research, institutions, and companies.

Space and Aerospace Research Institutions, Organizations, and Companies in Thiruvananthapuram

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre](/wiki/Vikram_Sarabhai_Space_Centre "Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre") (VSSC) | R&D Institution | ISRO's lead center for launch vehicle development. |
| [Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre](/wiki/Liquid_Propulsion_Systems_Centre "Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre") (LPSC) | R&D Institution | Develops liquid propulsion systems for rockets. |
| [ISRO Inertial Systems Unit](/wiki/ISRO_Inertial_Systems_Unit "ISRO Inertial Systems Unit") (IISU) | R&D Institution | Develops launch vehicle inertial systems, spacecraft inertial systems, inertial sensors evaluation and simulation, inertial system production, inertial systems electronic production etc. [[290]](#cite_note-290) |
| [Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station](/wiki/Thumba_Equatorial_Rocket_Launching_Station "Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station") (TERLS) | Spaceport | Located very close to Earth's magnetic equator. It is currently used for launching sounding rockets |
| [Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology](/wiki/Indian_Institute_of_Space_Science_and_Technology "Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology") (IIST) | Academic Institution | Aisa's first Space university.[[291]](#cite_note-291) Offers courses in space science and engineering. |
| [BrahMos Aerospace](/wiki/BrahMos_Aerospace "BrahMos Aerospace") | Defense Company | Produces the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile. |
| [Keltron](/wiki/Keltron "Keltron") | Electronics Company | Engages in space and defense electronics. |
| KEL Aerospace | Aerospace Company | Focuses on aerospace component manufacturing.[[292]](#cite_note-292) |
| [K-Space Trivandrum](/wiki/K-Space_Trivandrum "K-Space Trivandrum") | R&D Institution | Kerala government's space research initiative.[[289]](#cite_note-auto-289) |
| Hex 20 | Space-Tech Startup | Specializes in satellite technology.[[293]](#cite_note-293) |
| [Ananth Technologies](/wiki/Ananth_Technologies "Ananth Technologies") | Aerospace & Defense | Specializes in spacecraft systems, avionics, and satellite launch support for ISRO and other clients. |
| Strava Technologies | Aerospace & Defense | Provides electronic warfare, radar, and cybersecurity solutions to defense and aerospace sectors.[[294]](#cite_note-294) |
| Aeroprecision | Aerospace and Defense | Develop and Supplies high-precision aviation and military components, offering MRO and supply chain solutions.[[295]](#cite_note-295) |
| [Safran](/wiki/Safran "Safran") | Aerospace & Defense | A French multinational offering aircraft engines, avionics, and defense systems globally. |
| Kortas | Aerospace | Develops precision aerospace components, including propellant systems and tanks for launch vehicles.[[296]](#cite_note-296) |

## International relations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: International relations")]

Trivandrum is the location of the Consulate of the United Arab Emirates and the Consulate of the Maldives. The city also has the Honorary Consulates of Sri Lanka, Russia, and Germany, facilitating diplomatic and cultural engagements.

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Media")]

Main article: [Media in Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Media_in_Thiruvananthapuram "Media in Thiruvananthapuram")

Thiruvananthapuram has numerous newspaper publications, television and radio stations. Most of the media houses in [Kerala](/wiki/Media_in_Kerala "Media in Kerala") are based in Thiruvananthapuram. The first Malayalam channel, [Doordarshan Malayalam](/wiki/DD_Malayalam "DD Malayalam") began broadcasting from the city in 1981.[[302]](#cite_note-302) [Asianet](/wiki/Asianet_(TV_channel) "Asianet (TV channel)"), the first private channel in Malayalam, also started its telecasting from the city in 1993.[[303]](#cite_note-303) The other [Malayalam channels](/wiki/List_of_Malayalam-language_television_channels "List of Malayalam-language television channels") based in the city include [Asianet News](/wiki/Asianet_News "Asianet News"), [Amrita TV](/wiki/Amrita_TV "Amrita TV"), [Kappa TV](/wiki/Kappa_TV "Kappa TV"), [Kairali TV](/wiki/Kairali_TV "Kairali TV"), [Kairali We](/wiki/WE_TV "WE TV"), [Mathrubhumi News](/wiki/Mathrubhumi_News "Mathrubhumi News"), [Kaumudy TV](/wiki/Kaumudy_TV "Kaumudy TV"), [JaiHind TV](/wiki/JaiHind_TV "JaiHind TV"), [News18 Kerala](/wiki/News18 "News18") and People TV.[[304]](#cite_note-medialist-304) All major Malayalam channels, including [Asianet](/wiki/Asianet_(TV_channel) "Asianet (TV channel)"), [Janam TV](/wiki/Janam_TV "Janam TV"), [Jeevan TV](/wiki/Jeevan_TV "Jeevan TV"), [MediaOne TV](/wiki/MediaOne_TV "MediaOne TV") and [Manorama News](/wiki/Manorama_News "Manorama News") have production facilities or offices in the city.[[304]](#cite_note-medialist-304) TV channels are accessible via [cable subscription](/wiki/Cable_television "Cable television"), [direct-broadcast satellite services](/wiki/Direct-broadcast_satellite "Direct-broadcast satellite"), or [internet-based television](/wiki/IPTV "IPTV").[[305]](#cite_note-305)[[306]](#cite_note-306)[[307]](#cite_note-307) Prominent [Direct-to-Home](/wiki/Satellite_television "Satellite television") (DTH) entertainment services in Thiruvananthapuram include [Sun Direct DTH](/wiki/Sun_Direct_DTH "Sun Direct DTH"), [DD Direct+](/wiki/DD_Direct%2B "DD Direct+"), [Dish TV](/wiki/Dish_TV "Dish TV"), [Airtel digital TV](/wiki/Airtel_digital_TV "Airtel digital TV") and [Tata Play](/wiki/Tata_Sky "Tata Sky").

Major [Malayalam newspapers](/wiki/List_of_Malayalam-language_newspapers "List of Malayalam-language newspapers") available are *[Mathrubhumi](/wiki/Mathrubhumi "Mathrubhumi")*, *[Malayala Manorama](/wiki/Malayala_Manorama "Malayala Manorama")*, *[Kerala Kaumudi](/wiki/Kerala_Kaumudi "Kerala Kaumudi")*, *[Deshabhimani](/wiki/Deshabhimani "Deshabhimani")*, [Madhyamam](/wiki/Madhyamam "Madhyamam"), *[Janmabhumi](/wiki/Janmabhumi "Janmabhumi")*, *[Chandrika](/wiki/Chandrika_(newspaper) "Chandrika (newspaper)")*, *[Thejas](/wiki/Thejas "Thejas")*, *[Siraj Daily](/wiki/Siraj_Daily "Siraj Daily")*, *[Deepika](/wiki/Deepika_(newspaper) "Deepika (newspaper)")* and *[Rashtra Deepika](/wiki/Deepika_(newspaper)#Rashtra_Deepika "Deepika (newspaper)")*. The English language newspapers with editions from Thiruvananthapuram are *[The New Indian Express](/wiki/The_New_Indian_Express "The New Indian Express")*, *[The Hindu](/wiki/The_Hindu "The Hindu")*, *[The Deccan Chronicle](/wiki/The_Deccan_Chronicle "The Deccan Chronicle")* and *[The Times of India](/wiki/The_Times_of_India "The Times of India")*.[[304]](#cite_note-medialist-304)

[All India Radio](/wiki/All_India_Radio "All India Radio"), the national state-owned radio broadcaster, airs [Medium wave](/wiki/Medium_wave "Medium wave") and [Shortwave radio](/wiki/Shortwave_radio "Shortwave radio") stations in the city.[[308]](#cite_note-amradio-308)[[309]](#cite_note-309) The [Vividh Bharati](/wiki/Vividh_Bharati "Vividh Bharati") of All India Radio also airs an [FM radio](/wiki/FM_broadcasting "FM broadcasting") station known as Ananthapuri FM.[[310]](#cite_note-310)[[311]](#cite_note-311) Other FM radio channels broadcast from Thiruvananthapuram are [Big FM](/wiki/Big_FM_(Indian_radio_station) "Big FM (Indian radio station)") 92.7 MHz,[[312]](#cite_note-radiolist-312) [Club FM 94.3](/wiki/Club_FM_94.3 "Club FM 94.3") MHz,[[313]](#cite_note-Club_FM-313) [Radio Mirchi](/wiki/Radio_Mirchi "Radio Mirchi") 98.3 MHz,[[314]](#cite_note-Radio_Mirchi_Thiruvananthapuram-314) [Red FM 93.5](/wiki/Red_FM_93.5 "Red FM 93.5") MHz[[312]](#cite_note-radiolist-312) and [Radio DC](/wiki/Radio_DC "Radio DC") 90.4 MHz.[[315]](#cite_note-Radio_DC_Thiruvananthapuram-315)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Sports")]

Main article: [Sports in Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Sports_in_Thiruvananthapuram "Sports in Thiruvananthapuram")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6d/Greenfield_International_Stadium_Kerala_2.jpg/250px-Greenfield_International_Stadium_Kerala_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Greenfield_International_Stadium_Kerala_2.jpg)

Sports Hub (Greenfield Stadium) during the [India vs New Zealand T20](/wiki/New_Zealand_cricket_team_in_India_in_2017%E2%80%9318 "New Zealand cricket team in India in 2017–18")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/08/Chandrashekaran_nair_stadium_kerala.jpg/250px-Chandrashekaran_nair_stadium_kerala.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chandrashekaran_nair_stadium_kerala.jpg)

A football match in [CSN Stadium](/wiki/Chandrasekharan_Nair_Stadium "Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/07/BAI-U19-Ranking2016.jpg/250px-BAI-U19-Ranking2016.jpg)](/wiki/File:BAI-U19-Ranking2016.jpg)

Badminton at TOSS Academy

The most popular sports in Thiruvananthapuram are [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") and [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"). The city hosted the first international cricket match in Kerala at the [University Stadium](/wiki/University_Stadium_(Thiruvananthapuram) "University Stadium (Thiruvananthapuram)") in 1984.[[316]](#cite_note-1stodi-316) The city also hosted the first [Twenty20](/wiki/Twenty20 "Twenty20") International cricket match in Kerala.[[317]](#cite_note-317) The [Kerala Cricket Association](/wiki/Kerala_Cricket_Association "Kerala Cricket Association") is headquartered in Thiruvananthapuram.[[318]](#cite_note-318) Prominent cricketers from Thiruvananthapuram include [Sanju Samson](/wiki/Sanju_Samson "Sanju Samson"), [Raiphi Gomez](/wiki/Raiphi_Gomez "Raiphi Gomez"), [Ryan Ninan](/wiki/Ryan_Ninan "Ryan Ninan"), [Aneil Nambiar](/wiki/Aneil_Nambiar "Aneil Nambiar"), [K. N. Ananthapadmanabhan](/wiki/K._N._Ananthapadmanabhan "K. N. Ananthapadmanabhan"), [Rohan Prem](/wiki/Rohan_Prem "Rohan Prem"), [Udiramala Subramaniam](/wiki/Udiramala_Subramaniam "Udiramala Subramaniam"), [P. M. K. Mohandas](/wiki/P._M._K._Mohandas "P. M. K. Mohandas"), [Bhaskar Pillai](/wiki/Bhaskar_Pillai "Bhaskar Pillai") and [Padmanabhan Prasanth](/wiki/Padmanabhan_Prasanth "Padmanabhan Prasanth"). The Sports Hub, University Stadium,[[319]](#cite_note-319) [St Xavier's College Ground](/wiki/St_Xavier%27s_College_Ground "St Xavier's College Ground"),[[320]](#cite_note-320) KCA Cricket Stadium Mangalapuram,[[321]](#cite_note-321) Medical College ground[[322]](#cite_note-322) and [Vellyani Agricultural College Ground](/wiki/Vellyani_Agricultural_College_Ground "Vellyani Agricultural College Ground")[[323]](#cite_note-323) are the main cricket grounds in the city. [The Sports Hub, Trivandrum](/wiki/The_Sports_Hub,_Trivandrum "The Sports Hub, Trivandrum"), commonly known as Greenfield Stadium is one of the [largest cricket and football stadiums in India](/wiki/List_of_stadiums_in_India "List of stadiums in India").[[324]](#cite_note-grnfld-324) Thiruvananthapuram hosted the [2015 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2015_SAFF_Championship "2015 SAFF Championship") at the Greenfield Stadium.[[325]](#cite_note-325) [SBI Kerala](/wiki/SBI_Kerala_(Football_Club) "SBI Kerala (Football Club)"), [Titanium FC](/wiki/Titanium_FC "Titanium FC"), [KSEB](/wiki/Kerala_State_Electricity_Board "Kerala State Electricity Board"), Kovalam FC and Travancore Royals FC are the major football clubs based in Thiruvananthapuram.[[326]](#cite_note-326)[[327]](#cite_note-327) Football is usually played in the [Greenfield International Stadium (The Sports Hub)](/wiki/Greenfield_International_Stadium "Greenfield International Stadium"), [Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium](/wiki/Chandrasekharan_Nair_Stadium "Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium") and [University Stadium](/wiki/University_Stadium_(Thiruvananthapuram) "University Stadium (Thiruvananthapuram)").[[328]](#cite_note-328) Prominent football players from Thiruvananthapuram include [Jobby Justin](/wiki/Jobby_Justin "Jobby Justin").

The city has facilities to host most types of sports.[[329]](#cite_note-tvmsports-329) Thiruvananthapuram was one of the main venues for the [2015 National Games of India](/wiki/2015_National_Games_of_India "2015 National Games of India").[[330]](#cite_note-330) Athletic competitions are usually held at the [University Stadium](/wiki/University_Stadium_(Thiruvananthapuram) "University Stadium (Thiruvananthapuram)"), [Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium](/wiki/Chandrasekharan_Nair_Stadium "Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium") and [Central Stadium](/wiki/Central_Stadium_(Thiruvananthapuram) "Central Stadium (Thiruvananthapuram)").[[331]](#cite_note-331) There is a police-managed Albatros Swimming Pool underneath Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium.[[332]](#cite_note-332) The Trivandrum Marathon is a [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") organized by the Trivandrum runners club every year.[[333]](#cite_note-333) There will be two main races: a half marathon of 21 km and a full marathon of 42.19 km. A special 2 km fun run is also organized for public participation.[[334]](#cite_note-334) Trivand Run is another marathon conducted every January in the city.[[335]](#cite_note-335)

[Jimmy George Indoor Stadium](/wiki/Jimmy_George_Indoor_Stadium "Jimmy George Indoor Stadium") is a major indoor stadium in the state. It is used for conducting [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball"), [volleyball](/wiki/Volleyball "Volleyball"), [table tennis](/wiki/Table_tennis "Table tennis"), [gymnastics](/wiki/Gymnastics "Gymnastics"), [aquatics](/wiki/List_of_water_sports "List of water sports") and [martial arts](/wiki/Martial_arts "Martial arts").[[329]](#cite_note-tvmsports-329) The stadium has the first altitude-simulated training facility in South India, known as Astra.[[336]](#cite_note-336) The major sports training and coaching institutions include the [Lakshmibai National College of Physical Education](/wiki/Lakshmibai_National_College_of_Physical_Education "Lakshmibai National College of Physical Education") (LNCPE), TOSS Academy and the Tenvic Sports Coaching Academy at the Sports Hub.[[337]](#cite_note-337)

Basketball tournaments are usually conducted by the schools in the city.[[338]](#cite_note-338) Thiruvananthapuram hosted the 61st National Shooting Championship at the Vattiyoorkavu Shooting Range.[[339]](#cite_note-339) [Surfing](/wiki/Surfing "Surfing") is also a popular sport on the beaches. Many surfing and [standup paddleboarding](/wiki/Standup_paddleboarding "Standup paddleboarding") tournaments are held in the city.[[340]](#cite_note-340) The surf competitions are usually held on [Kovalam Beach](/wiki/Kovalam_Beach "Kovalam Beach") and [Varkala Beach](/wiki/Varkala_Beach "Varkala Beach").[[341]](#cite_note-341) [Paragliding](/wiki/Paragliding "Paragliding") is another [adventure sport](/wiki/Extreme_sport "Extreme sport") usually seen on Varkala Beach.[[342]](#cite_note-342)

The [SAI Trivandrum golf club](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram_Golf_Club "Thiruvananthapuram Golf Club"), established in 1850, is one of the oldest golf courses in India.[[343]](#cite_note-saigolf-343) It is leased to the [Sports Authority of India](/wiki/Sports_Authority_of_India "Sports Authority of India").[[343]](#cite_note-saigolf-343)

Professional sports clubs based in the city

| Trivandrum Tuskers[[344]](#cite_note-344) | Cricket | SE Premier League | Trivandrum International Stadium | 2016 |
| Trivandrum Royals[[345]](#cite_note-345) | Cricket | Kerala Premier League | Trivandrum International Stadium | 2015 |
| [Thiruvananthapuram Kombans FC](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram_Kombans_FC "Thiruvananthapuram Kombans FC")[[346]](#cite_note-346) | Football | [Super Kerala League](/w/index.php?title=Super_Kerala_League&action=edit&redlink=1 "Super Kerala League (page does not exist)") | Chandrasekharan Nair Stadium | 2024 |

## Defense and security forces

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thiruvananthapuram&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Defense and security forces")]

Defense and Security Forces in Trivandrum

| Force | Description | Location |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Indian Army](/wiki/Indian_Army "Indian Army") | The 40th Brigade of the Indian Army is headquartered in Trivandrum, handling defense operations and security in Kerala and Tamil Nadu. | Trivandrum[[348]](#cite_note-348) |
| [Indian Air Force](/wiki/Indian_Air_Force "Indian Air Force") | The [Southern Air Command](/wiki/Southern_Air_Command_(India) "Southern Air Command (India)") (SAC) of the Indian Air Force (IAF) is headquartered in Trivandrum. It was established on 19 July 1984 to enhance India's air presence over the Indian Ocean. | Trivandrum |
| [Indian Navy](/wiki/Indian_Navy "Indian Navy") | The Indian Navy is setting up a strategic center in Trivandrum, including a Naval Armament Depot and Naval Communication Centre to strengthen coastal security. | Trivandrum[[349]](#cite_note-349) |
| [Border Security Force](/wiki/Border_Security_Force "Border Security Force") (BSF) | The Sector Headquarters (SHQ) BSF Trivandrum is located in Muttathara, near the international airport, overseeing BSF operations in the region. | Trivandrum[[350]](#cite_note-350) |
| [Central Reserve Police Force](/wiki/Central_Reserve_Police_Force "Central Reserve Police Force") (CRPF) | The CRPF Kerala Sector Headquarters and the 164th Battalion are stationed in Trivandrum, responsible for maintaining law and order. | Trivandrum[[351]](#cite_note-351) |
| [Indian Coast Guard](/wiki/Indian_Coast_Guard "Indian Coast Guard") | The Indian Coast Guard station in Trivandrum is responsible for coastal security, surveillance, and maritime operations. | Trivandrum[[352]](#cite_note-352) |
| [Central Industrial Security Force](/wiki/Central_Industrial_Security_Force "Central Industrial Security Force") (CISF) | The CISF unit in Trivandrum secures key installations, including Trivandrum International Airport, VSSC, and LPSC. | Trivandrum[[353]](#cite_note-353) |
| [Sashastra Seema Bal](/wiki/Sashastra_Seema_Bal "Sashastra Seema Bal") | A battalion headquarters of the [Sashastra Seema Bal](/wiki/Sashastra_Seema_Bal "Sashastra Seema Bal") (SSB), a Central Armed Police Force under the Ministry of Home Affairs, is proposed to be established in Trivandrum as part of a major defence expansion to strengthen security infrastructure in southern India. | Trivandrum[[354]](#cite_note-354) |
| Kerala State Industrial Security Force (KSISF) | The KSISF is a state agency tasked with securing key industrial and infrastructure installations. | Trivandrum[[355]](#cite_note-355) |
| [Kerala Police](/wiki/Kerala_Police "Kerala Police") | The [Thiruvananthapuram City Police](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram_City_Police "Thiruvananthapuram City Police") Commissionerate serves as the headquarters, overseeing law enforcement across the city. | Trivandrum[[356]](#cite_note-356) |
